area_description: Memory in LLMs addresses the challenge of maintaining and utilizing information across interactions, and help users recall past multi-modal memories.

categories.name: Memory Recall
categories.description: Retrieve from past interactions and logs to answer the users' recall questions. Challenges include generating answers across different memories and handling dense, highly similar memories.

sub_topics.name: Sparse Memory QA
sub_topics.description: Past memory is fairly sparse. Generating answers across different sparse memories can still be challenging.

sub_topics.name: Dense Memory QA
sub_topics.description: Past memory is dense, highly similar with many duplicates. Retrieval, noise reduction, and deduplication are even more challenging.

categories.name: Memory Organization
categories.description: Best way to organize past memory for recall QA and personalized conversation. Includes linear, layered, graph-based, and internalized memory structures.

sub_topics.name: Linear Memory
sub_topics.description: Focus on when to discard and update memory. RL can be leveraged to make decisions to maintain a clean and compact memory.

sub_topics.name: Layered Memory
sub_topics.description: Multiple layers such as core (profile), semantic (factual), episodic (associated with time/location). Also covers retrieval granularity: session-level, turn-level, and topic-level.

sub_topics.name: Tree/Graph-based Memory
sub_topics.description: Mimic human associative memory, making it easier to hop from one memory to another related by topic, location, event, person, etc.

sub_topics.name: Memory Internalization
sub_topics.description: Store personal memories into model parameters through LoRA, embedding feeding, and other parameter-level methods.
